content: Obesity is a chronic, relapsing, multifactorial disease characterized by abnormal or excessive fat accumulation that impairs health. BMI classification: normal 18.5-24.9 kg/m², overweight 25-29.9, obesity class I 30-34.9, class II 35-39.9, class III ≥40. BMI limitations: doesn't distinguish fat from muscle mass, doesn't assess fat distribution (visceral vs subcutaneous). Alternative measures: waist circumference (>102cm men, >88cm women indicates abdominal obesity), waist-to-hip ratio, body fat percentage (DEXA, bioimpedance). Pathophysiology involves complex interaction of genetic, epigenetic, behavioral, environmental, and socioeconomic factors. Adipose tissue functions as endocrine organ secreting adipokines (leptin, adiponectin). Obesity associated with increased risk of T2DM, CVD, certain cancers, osteoarthritis, sleep apnea, depression.
